sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Rare Network requests Cardano treasury funding for Cardano title sponsorship of Rare Evo 2026 and Rare Dev Gov Day 2026 in Las Vegas.

Deliverables: Cardano title sponsorship of the Rare Evo 2026 main stage and global livestream, Cardano title sponsorship of Rare Dev Gov Day 2026 on July 28, 2026 at ARIA Las Vegas, Free General Admission access for the Cardano community, Complimentary booth space for selected Cardano ecosystem startup projects, Five Cardano-focused main stage panelist positions, Breakout room and workshop space, Livestream Cardano ads and global livestream participation, Tier 1 media coverage for Cardano ecosystem announcements, Travel and lodging support for 20 Cardano community members, Return of 20% of all VIP ticket sales to the Cardano Treasury

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Rare Network requests ₳2,750,000, described as $660,000 at a $0.24 ADA valuation, to cover Cardano sponsorship packages for Rare Evo 2026 and Cardano Dev Gov Day 2026.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal allocates ₳1,166,666.67 for Cardano Dev Gov Day 2026 sponsorship, ₳833,333.33 for Rare Evo 2026 title sponsorship, and ₳750,000 for Intersect, audit, fees, tax withholdings, and a Dev Gov Day 2027 deposit.

Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Rare Network states it will return 20% of all VIP ticket sales to the Cardano Treasury, with the current retail value of a VIP ticket listed as $900.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Rare Evo 2026 will occur regardless of treasury funding, but the proposal says treasury funding would expand Cardano's presence, accessibility, participation, and strategic industry outreach.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): Rare Network claims it has operated in the Cardano ecosystem since 2020 and has run Rare Evo annually for five consecutive years.

Claim (adoption, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal reports Rare Evo 2025 metrics including 2,500 annual attendees, 80 exhibitor booths, 85 speakers, 100+ ecosystem partners, 20K+ livestream views, 4M social impressions, and 40+ Cardano projects represented.
missing: Independent evidence for: Rare Network states it will return 20% of all VIP ticket sales to the Cardano Treasury, with the current retail value of a VIP ticket listed as $900.

Independent evidence for: Rare Evo 2026 will occur regardless of treasury funding, but the proposal says treasury funding would expand Cardano's presence, accessibility, participation, and strategic industry outreach.
Independent evidence for: Rare Network claims it has operated in the Cardano ecosystem since 2020 and has run Rare Evo annually for five consecutive years.

Independent evidence for: The proposal claims existing venue relationships, sponsor networks, production capabilities, volunteer coordination, and industry partnerships reduce execution risk and enable professional scale.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Rare Network requests ₳2,750,000, described as $660,000 at a $0.24 ADA valuation, to cover Cardano sponsorship packages for Rare Evo 2026 and Cardano Dev Gov Day 2026." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Rare Network states it will return 20% of all VIP ticket sales to the Cardano Treasury, with the current retail value of a VIP ticket listed as $900." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
